chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَحِلُّ لِلْمَمْلُوكِ النَّظَرُ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ مَوْلَاتِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 167 – What is Permissible for the slaves to look at from his mistress

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/367)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/367)

parts.text: عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ

text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْمَمْلُوكِ يَرَى شَعْرَ مَوْلَاتِهِ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Abdullah and Ahmad, two sons of Muhammad Bin Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Aban Bin Usman, from Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Abdullah who said,
‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the slave who sees the hair of his mistress. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from ‘Abd Allah and Ahmad sons of Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn abu ‘Abd Allah who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a slave’s looking at the hairs of the mistress. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not unlawful.’”
